skill.name: Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright (.NET)
skill.description: Management plane SDK for provisioning and managing Microsoft Playwright Testing workspaces via Azure Resource Manager in .NET. Use for creating workspaces, checking name availability, and managing workspace quotas.
skill.summary: Provision and manage Azure Playwright Testing workspaces using the Azure Resource Manager SDK in .NET
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill containing documentation and guidance for using the Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright .NET SDK. No executable code is present. The skill provides management plane operations documentation (creating workspaces, checking quotas, name availability). Static analysis scanned 0 files (0 lines) with no suspicious patterns detected. Risk score is 0/100.

content.value_statement: Automate the provisioning and management of Microsoft Playwright Testing workspaces in Azure using the .NET Resource Manager SDK. This skill enables CI/CD pipelines and DevOps workflows to create, update, and monitor Playwright Testing resources without manual Azure portal configuration.
actual_capabilities: Create new Playwright Testing workspaces in Azure Resource Manager
Check name availability for workspace names before creation
List and retrieve existing workspaces within subscriptions and resource groups
Update workspace properties including tags and configuration
Query subscription-level and workspace-level quota information
Delete workspaces and manage their lifecycle
limitations: This skill provides management plane operations only, not test execution
Does not include test running capabilities - use Azure.Developer.MicrosoftPlaywrightTesting.NUnit for that
Requires valid Azure subscription and appropriate RBAC permissions
No built-in retry logic or advanced resilience patterns

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer Automation
use_cases.description: Automate Playwright Testing workspace provisioning as part of CI/CD pipelines. Create workspaces on-demand for test environments and clean them up after tests complete.
use_cases.target_user: DevOps engineers building automated testing pipelines

use_cases.title: Azure Administrator
use_cases.description: Manage multiple Playwright Testing workspaces across subscriptions. Check quotas, update configurations, and monitor workspace provisioning states.
use_cases.target_user: Azure administrators managing cloud testing infrastructure

use_cases.title: Developer Self-Service
use_cases.description: Enable developers to quickly provision dedicated Playwright Testing workspaces for their projects without needing direct Azure portal access.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers needing on-demand testing infrastructure

prompt_templates.title: Create a new Playwright workspace
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a new Azure Playwright Testing workspace named 'my-workspace' in the 'test-rg' resource group using the Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright SDK. Include tags for Team=QA and Environment=Staging.

prompt_templates.title: Check workspace name availability
prompt_templates.prompt: Write C# code to check if the workspace name 'my-playwright-tests' is available in our subscription using Azure Resource Manager. Show how to handle both available and unavailable cases.

prompt_templates.title: List all workspaces in subscription
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to list all Playwright Testing workspaces in our Azure subscription and display their current provisioning state, location, and data plane URI.
prompt_templates.scenario: Inventory and monitoring of existing workspaces

prompt_templates.title: Get quota information
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I query the available quota for parallel test execution in our subscription? I need to check both subscription-level and workspace-level quotas using the Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright SDK.

output_examples.input: Create a Playwright workspace in West US 3 with regional affinity enabled
output_examples.output:
To create a workspace with regional affinity enabled:

1. Define the workspace data with PlaywrightWorkspaceData
2. Set RegionalAffinity property to PlaywrightRegionalAffinity.Enabled
3. Use CreateOrUpdateAsync with WaitUntil.Completed

The workspace will be created in West US 3 with regional affinity, which ensures test traffic stays within the specified region for compliance or latency requirements.

output_examples.input: What's the difference between this SDK and the test execution SDK?
output_examples.output:
Two different SDKs serve different purposes:

- Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright: Management plane - create workspaces, manage quotas, check name availability
- Azure.Developer.MicrosoftPlaywrightTesting.NUnit: Test execution - run Playwright tests at scale on cloud browsers

This skill handles the management/infrastructure side. Use the other SDK to actually run your Playwright tests.

best_practices: Always use DefaultAzureCredential for authentication instead of hardcoding keys or secrets
Use WaitUntil.Completed for create/update/delete operations to ensure they finish before proceeding
Store the DataplaneUri returned from workspace creation for configuring test execution SDKs
anti_patterns: Using hardcoded Azure credentials instead of managed identity or DefaultAzureCredential
Ignoring RequestFailedException error handling for ARM API failures
Attempting to run Playwright tests using this management SDK instead of the test execution SDK

faq.question: What is Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright used for?
faq.answer: This SDK manages Playwright Testing workspaces in Azure. It handles the management plane: creating workspaces, checking name availability, managing quotas, and updating workspace properties. It does NOT run tests.

faq.question: How do I run Playwright tests after creating a workspace?
faq.answer: After creating a workspace, use the Azure.Developer.MicrosoftPlaywrightTesting.NUnit package to run tests. Set the PLAYWRIGHT_SERVICE_URL environment variable to the DataplaneUri from your workspace.

faq.question: What authentication method should I use?
faq.answer: Always use DefaultAzureCredential. It automatically handles authentication via managed identity, Visual Studio credentials, Azure CLI, or environment variables without hardcoding secrets.

faq.question: Can I use this skill to manage existing workspaces?
faq.answer: Yes, you can use GetAsync to retrieve existing workspaces, ExistsAsync to check if a workspace exists, and UpdateAsync to modify workspace properties like tags.

faq.question: What are the key workspace properties I need to know?
faq.answer: The most important properties are: DataplaneUri (for test execution configuration), WorkspaceId (unique identifier), RegionalAffinity (enable/disable), LocalAuth (token-based access), and ProvisioningState (creation status).

faq.question: How do I handle workspace creation failures?
faq.answer: Catch RequestFailedException and check the Status code. Common codes: 409 (already exists), 400 (bad request), 403 (forbidden), 404 (resource group not found). Handle each case appropriately in your code.
